message:
fix(cli): schema-adaptive reservation queries for databases without released_ts column

The file_reservations table may lack the legacy released_ts column in
newer database schemas that rely exclusively on the sidecar release
ledger (file_reservation_releases). Previously, all SQL queries
unconditionally referenced fr.released_ts, causing runtime errors on
databases where that column was never created.

Add has_file_reservations_released_ts_column() which introspects the
schema via PRAGMA table_info. Thread this boolean through
legacy_active_reservation_predicate_sql(),
active_reservation_filter_sql(), and reservation_released_ts_sql() so
they emit correct SQL for each schema variant:

- Both ledger + legacy column: COALESCE with AND (existing behavior)
- Ledger only, no legacy column: use only release ledger predicates
- Legacy column only, no ledger: use only released_ts predicates
- Neither: return safe fallback ("1 = 1" / "NULL")

The navigate route for active-only reservations now uses the computed
released_ts SQL expression in the SELECT clause instead of the raw
column reference, preventing "no such column" errors.

Tests:
- active_reservation_helpers_omit_legacy_column_when_schema_lacks_it
- build_navigate active-only reservations with release ledger but no
  legacy released_ts column

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
